topic: Light Waves: Refraction, Total Internal Reflection & Critical Angle

subUnits.title: 9.1 Refraction, Snell's Law, Real & Apparent Depth, and Total Internal Reflection
notes: Refraction, Snell's Law, and Total Internal Reflection
Key Concepts:
1. Refraction of Light:
- Bending of light when it passes from one medium to another of different optical density
- Caused by change in speed of light in different media
- Light bends toward normal when entering denser medium, away from normal when entering rarer medium
2. Laws of Refraction:
a) Incident ray, refracted ray, and normal lie in the same plane
b) Snell's Law: sin i / sin r = n (constant for a given pair of media)
- n = refractive index = speed in air / speed in medium
3. Real and Apparent Depth:
- Real depth: actual depth of object in medium
- Apparent depth: perceived depth due to refraction
- Formula: n = real depth / apparent depth
4. Total Internal Reflection (TIR):
- Complete reflection of light back into denser medium when angle of incidence exceeds critical angle
- Conditions: light travels from denser to rarer medium, angle of incidence > critical angle
- Critical angle: angle of incidence where angle of refraction = 90°
5. Applications in Nigeria:
- Mirage formation on hot Nigerian highways
- Optical fiber communication in Nigerian telecom networks
- Fish visibility in rivers and ponds
Conclusion:
- Refraction bends light due to speed change; governed by Snell's Law
- Real/apparent depth relates to refractive index
- TIR occurs when incidence > critical angle in denser-to-rarer transition
- Mastery supports optics, telecommunications, and atmospheric physics

opts: Light changes speed in different media
quiz.explanation: Refraction is caused by a change in light's speed when moving between media of different optical densities.
Snell's Law: sin i / sin r = refractive index (n) for a given pair of media.

quiz.q: When light enters a denser medium, it:
quiz.explanation: Light bends toward the normal when entering a denser medium due to decreased speed.

quiz.q: The relationship between real depth, apparent depth, and refractive index is:
quiz.explanation: Refractive index n = real depth / apparent depth.

opts: Light travels from rarer to denser
Light travels from denser to rarer and i > critical angle
quiz.explanation: TIR requires light moving from denser to rarer medium with angle of incidence greater than critical angle.

quiz.q: In Nigerian context, mirages on highways are caused by:
opts: Refraction and TIR in hot air layers
quiz.explanation: Highway mirages result from refraction and total internal reflection in layers of hot, less dense air near the road.

quiz.q: Critical angle is defined as the angle of incidence where:
quiz.explanation: Critical angle is the incidence angle at which refraction angle becomes 90°.

quiz.q: A common misconception about refraction is:
opts: Light bends due to speed change
Light always bends away from normal
quiz.explanation: Refraction is caused by speed change, not just wavelength change, and occurs in all transparent media.

quiz.q: Optical fibers in Nigerian telecom rely on:
quiz.explanation: Optical fibers transmit data using total internal reflection within the glass core.

quiz.q: To calculate apparent depth, you should:
quiz.explanation: Apparent depth = real depth / refractive index (n).
